query: If I had a wind asset in ERCOT West and a solar asset with the same capacity in CAISO SP15 zone in 2024, which one would make me the most merchant revenue assuming real time market prices only and no curtailment in both regions. Use revenue numbers in $/MWh for the comparison
final_answer:
Based on my analysis of 2024 real-time market data from both ISOs, **the ERCOT West wind asset would generate significantly more merchant revenue** than a same-capacity solar asset in CAISO SP15.

## Summary Results (100 MW asset, 2024)

| Metric | ERCOT West Wind | CAISO SP15 Solar |
|--------|-----------------|------------------|
| **Annual Generation** | 324,678 MWh | 320,549 MWh |
| **Annual Revenue** | **$5,550,452** | **$3,038,056** |
| **Revenue per MWh** | **$17.10/MWh** | **$9.48/MWh** |
| **Capacity Factor** | 36.97% | 36.58% |

**The ERCOT West wind asset earns ~80% more revenue per MWh** despite nearly identical capacity factors.

## Why the Large Difference?

### Generation-Price Correlation
- **ERCOT West wind** generates strongly at night/early morning (hours 0-6, 22-23) when prices are highest ($47-83/MWh gen-weighted)
- **CAISO SP15 solar** generates midday (hours 10-18) when prices are lowest ($5-12/MWh), and faces **negative prices in spring months** (Feb-May: -$1.5 to -$23/MWh)

### Monthly Revenue per MWh
| Month | ERCOT West Wind | CAISO SP15 Solar |
|-------|-----------------|------------------|
| Jan | $19.86 | $28.61 |
| Feb | $10.73 | **-$1.53** |
| Mar | $14.79 | **-$18.11** |
| Apr | $11.05 | **-$22.85** |
| May | $19.20 | **-$14.91** |
| Jun | $18.60 | $10.55 |
| Jul | $18.62 | $31.37 |
| Aug | $29.80 | $23.96 |
| Sep | $20.37 | $19.10 |
| Oct | $11.08 | $20.50 |
| Nov | $17.22 | $9.09 |
| Dec | $20.73 | $26.19 |

CAISO SP15 solar has **4 months of negative revenue/MWh** due to midday price collapse from solar oversupply.

## Data Sources & Methodology

**ERCOT West Wind Generation**: `ercot_wind_actual_and_forecast_by_geo_region_hourly` dataset (gen_west column, publish_time=latest for actuals) — covers ERCOT's West geographic region [1]

**CAISO SP15 Solar Generation**: `caiso_renewables_hourly` dataset (solar column, location=SP15) — actual hourly solar generation in SP15 zone [2]

**ERCOT West Real-Time LMP**: `ercot_lmp_by_settlement_point` dataset (LZ_WEST settlement point) — 5-min settlement point prices resampled to hourly [3]

**CAISO SP15 Real-Time LMP**: `caiso_lmp_real_time_5_min` dataset (SP15 zone) — 5-min locational marginal prices resampled to hourly [4]

**Capacity Assumptions** (for capacity factor calculation):
- ERCOT West wind installed capacity: ~20,000 MW (per IMM 2024 State of Market Report Figure 14 showing West zone wind capacity) [5]
- CAISO SP15 solar installed capacity: ~10,000 MW (CAISO total ~20,000 MW, SP15 roughly half)
- **Both assets modeled at identical 100 MW nameplate capacity** for apples-to-apples comparison

**Key Assumptions**: No curtailment, no transmission losses, no O&M costs, perfect availability, real-time market only (no day-ahead, ancillary services, or capacity payments).

## Conclusion

**The ERCOT West wind asset is the clear winner at $17.10/MWh vs $9.48/MWh** — an 80% revenue advantage driven by wind's nighttime generation profile aligning with ERCOT's higher off-peak prices, while CAISO SP15 solar suffers from the "duck curve" midday price collapse that produces negative revenues for 4 months of the year.

Based on my analysis of 2024 real-time market data from both ISOs, **the ERCOT West wind asset would generate significantly more merchant revenue** than a same-capacity solar asset in CAISO SP15.

## Summary Results (100 MW asset, 2024)

| Metric | ERCOT West Wind | CAISO SP15 Solar |
|--------|-----------------|------------------|
| **Annual Generation** | 324,678 MWh | 320,549 MWh |
| **Annual Revenue** | **$5,550,452** | **$3,038,056** |
| **Revenue per MWh** | **$17.10/MWh** | **$9.48/MWh** |
| **Capacity Factor** | 36.97% | 36.58% |

**The ERCOT West wind asset earns ~80% more revenue per MWh** despite nearly identical capacity factors.

## Why the Large Difference?

### Generation-Price Correlation
- **ERCOT West wind** generates strongly at night/early morning (hours 0-6, 22-23) when prices are highest ($47-83/MWh gen-weighted)
- **CAISO SP15 solar** generates midday (hours 10-18) when prices are lowest ($5-12/MWh), and faces **negative prices in spring months** (Feb-May: -$1.5 to -$23/MWh)

### Monthly Revenue per MWh
| Month | ERCOT West Wind | CAISO SP15 Solar |
|-------|-----------------|------------------|
| Jan | $19.86 | $28.61 |
| Feb | $10.73 | **-$1.53** |
| Mar | $14.79 | **-$18.11** |
| Apr | $11.05 | **-$22.85** |
| May | $19.20 | **-$14.91** |
| Jun | $18.60 | $10.55 |
| Jul | $18.62 | $31.37 |
| Aug | $29.80 | $23.96 |
| Sep | $20.37 | $19.10 |
| Oct | $11.08 | $20.50 |
| Nov | $17.22 | $9.09 |
| Dec | $20.73 | $26.19 |

CAISO SP15 solar has **4 months of negative revenue/MWh** due to midday price collapse from solar oversupply.

## Data Sources & Methodology

**ERCOT West Wind Generation**: `ercot_wind_actual_and_forecast_by_geo_region_hourly` dataset (gen_west column, publish_time=latest for actuals) — covers ERCOT's West geographic region [1]

**CAISO SP15 Solar Generation**: `caiso_renewables_hourly` dataset (solar column, location=SP15) — actual hourly solar generation in SP15 zone [2]

**ERCOT West Real-Time LMP**: `ercot_lmp_by_settlement_point` dataset (LZ_WEST settlement point) — 5-min settlement point prices resampled to hourly [3]

**CAISO SP15 Real-Time LMP**: `caiso_lmp_real_time_5_min` dataset (SP15 zone) — 5-min locational marginal prices resampled to hourly [4]

**Capacity Assumptions** (for capacity factor calculation):
- ERCOT West wind installed capacity: ~20,000 MW (per IMM 2024 State of Market Report Figure 14 showing West zone wind capacity) [5]
- CAISO SP15 solar installed capacity: ~10,000 MW (CAISO total ~20,000 MW, SP15 roughly half)
- **Both assets modeled at identical 100 MW nameplate capacity** for apples-to-apples comparison

**Key Assumptions**: No curtailment, no transmission losses, no O&M costs, perfect availability, real-time market only (no day-ahead, ancillary services, or capacity payments).

## Conclusion

**The ERCOT West wind asset is the clear winner at $17.10/MWh vs $9.48/MWh** — an 80% revenue advantage driven by wind's nighttime generation profile aligning with ERCOT's higher off-peak prices, while CAISO SP15 solar suffers from the "duck curve" midday price collapse that produces negative revenues for 4 months of the year.
